content.claim: As of June 2026, Education Minister Lannert Judit explicitly stated that the current admissions system produces significant social selection and places heavy burden on children and families, while denying any proposal for outright abolition and calling for societal dialogue on how the admissions system functions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The specific mechanism by which Minister Lannert proposes to reform admissions remains undefined
The statement was politically reframed by Fidesz faction leadership as a proposal for total abolition, suggesting significant interpretive dispute over policy intent
No timeline or implementation pathway for dialogue is specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
